normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Strategy
version_date: February 2016; Version 1 endorsed by WAPC with modifications April 2016
scope_summary: Strategic land-use planning framework for the City of Melville, prepared in conjunction with Local Planning Scheme No. 6 and providing the strategic basis for that scheme over the period 2012-2031 and beyond. Part 1 sets the vision, planning objectives, strategies/actions and Strategic Development Areas (including activity centres, transit corridors and Canning Bridge); Part 2 contains background information and analysis of the state, regional and local planning context. It guides where residential intensification, activity centres, open space, transport and infrastructure change should occur but does not itself contain development assessment standards.

quality_flags: Document dated February 2016; only version-control evidence in text is the single line 'Version 1 April 2016 Endorsed by WAPC with modifications' - no later consolidation markers found, so currency should be confirmed against the City of Melville website
Table of contents dot leaders and repeated page headers/footers are interleaved in the extracted text (cosmetic only)
Strategy cites the superseded SPP 3.1 R-Codes naming (2010-era policy framework), reflecting its 2016 drafting date
